name: Andre Black's Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease and flour three 8-inch layer pans.
3. In a large bowl, combine eggs, sugar, and oil. Beat on high speed until creamy and thick, about 3-5 minutes.
4. Sift together flour, cinnamon, salt, and baking soda.
5. Gradually add 1/3 of the dry ingredients to the egg mixture; beat well.
6. Repeat with remaining dry ingredients, beating well after each addition. The batter will be extremely stiff and may need to be blended with a spatula or spoon.
7. Fold in carrots, coconut, pineapple, walnuts, and raisins one by one.
8. Divide batter between the three prepared cake pans.
9. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 40-45 minutes, arranging cake pans once in the oven.
10. The cake is done when a tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Let cool in pans for 5 minutes before turning out onto a rack to cool completely.
11. Prepare frosting by combining margarine, cream cheese, sugar, and vanilla. Beat until smooth.
12. Frost the cooled cake layers with the frosting.
